summary: American novelist, journalist, and public intellectual known for The Naked and the Dead, The Armies of the Night, The Executioner's Song, and the essay 'The White Negro.' A central figure of New Journalism, known for his pugnacious personality, feuds with Gore Vidal and Kate Millett, and the stabbing of his wife Adele Morales.

works.name: The Naked and the Dead
works.description: Mailer's first novel, based on his experience fighting in the Philippines in World War II. Depicts an American regiment's invasion of the fictional Filipino island Anopopei. Uses the euphemism 'fug' instead of the actual word, prompting Dorothy Parker to call Mailer 'the man who can't spell fuck.' Sergeant Croft orders an attack on Mount Anaka, crushes a baby bird, and kills a prisoner-of-war. A character poops his pants during the opening action scene and is later killed by a hand grenade.

clues.clue: About American regiment's invasion of fictional Filipino island Anopopei; uses euphemism 'fug'

clues.clue: Based on Mailer's WWII experience in the Philippines
examples: expanded upon his experience fighting in the Philippines in World War II
drew on his real war experience

works.name: The Armies of the Night
works.description: A nonfiction novel about Mailer's attendance at the 1967 March on the Pentagon. Split into two sections titled 'History as a Novel' and 'The Novel as History.' Mailer joins his friend Mitch Goodman at the event and marches alongside luminaries like Dwight Macdonald and Robert Lowell. The book includes a lengthy analysis of anti-Vietnam War protests.

clues.clue: About the 1967 March on the Pentagon; split into 'History as a Novel' and 'The Novel as History'
examples: wrote a novel about his attendance at the March on the Pentagon
two sections are titled 'History as a Novel' and 'The Novel as History'

examples: describes attending a Washington, D.C., event with his friend Mitch Goodman
recounts participating in a 1967 march alongside such luminaries as Dwight Macdonald and Robert Lowell

works.name: The Executioner's Song
works.description: A Pulitzer Prize-winning nonfiction novel about the capital punishment of Gary Gilmore in Utah. Gilmore murders two men and then demands his death penalty be delivered as soon as possible. Nicole Baker has a brief affair with Gilmore. The novel begins with Toni and Brenda helping Gilmore get back on his feet in Utah.

clues.clue: About the execution of Gary Gilmore in Utah; Gilmore demands his death penalty
examples: a Pulitzer winning account of Utah administering the death penalty to killer Gary Gilmore
Gary Gilmore, a man who murders two men and then demands his death penalty be delivered

clues.clue: Nicole Baker has affair with Gilmore; begins with Toni and Brenda helping him in Utah
examples: Nicole Baker has a brief affair with Gary Gilmore
begins with Toni and Brenda agreeing to help the main character get back on his feet in Utah

works.name: "The White Negro"
works.description: An essay with the subtitle 'Superficial Reflections on the Hipster.' It discusses how existentialist hipsters ('philosophical psychopaths') 'absorbed the existentialist synapses of black men' after World War II. Opens with a discussion of the 'psychic havoc' of WWII. Describes jazz as the 'music of orgasm.' Popularized the term 'hipster' for white jazz aficionados. Posits a choice between death in state-sponsored violence (atomic bomb) and the slow death of conformity. CIA records show the agency kept a copy on file. Eventually collected in Advertisements for Myself. James Baldwin criticized Mailer's use of the language of 'deprived Negroes.'

clues.clue: 'Superficial Reflections on the Hipster'; describes hipster as 'philosophical psychopath' absorbing black culture
examples: wrote some 'superficial reflections on the hipster' in his essay 'The White Negro'
posits a bleak choice between death in the state-sponsored violence of the atomic bomb and the slow death of conformity

clues.clue: Opens with discussion of 'psychic havoc' of WWII; jazz is 'music of orgasm'
examples: begins with a discussion of the 'psychic havoc' of World War II
Mailer's description of jazz as the 'music of orgasm'

clues.clue: Collected in Advertisements for Myself; CIA kept a copy
examples: appeared in its author's Advertisements for Myself
CIA records reveal that the agency kept on file a copy

works.name: An American Dream
works.description: A novel in which Stephen Rojack murders his estranged wife Deborah and then has anal sex with her maid. The first paragraph mentions Robert Kennedy's friendship with the protagonist. Rojack visits Deborah after getting sick at a cocktail party. Kate Millett attacked the patriarchal biases of this novel in Sexual Politics.

clues.clue: Stephen Rojack murders his wife and has sex with her maid; first paragraph mentions Robert Kennedy
examples: Stephen Rojack murders his estranged wife and then has anal sex with her maid
first paragraph mentions Robert Kennedy's friendship with the protagonist

works.name: Ancient Evenings
works.description: A historical fiction novel set in ancient Egypt about Menenhetet, a man who gets reincarnated three times. On the first occasion, he tells of his time serving as charioteer for Ramses II at the Battle of Kadesh. During the 'Night of the Pig,' he tells his life story. He is undone by the High Priest Khem-Usha. The novel discusses the indigestible phallus of Osiris and incorporates the Egyptian Book of the Dead. Orhan Pamuk was accused of plagiarizing this novel for My Name is Red.

clues.clue: Set in ancient Egypt; Menenhetet reincarnated three times; serves as charioteer for Ramses II at Battle of Kadesh
examples: set in Ancient Egypt, about Menenhetet, a man who gets reincarnated three times

clues.clue: Incorporates Egyptian Book of the Dead; influenced Burroughs's The Western Lands
examples: this author's incorporation of The Book of the Dead into his novel Ancient Evenings

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Mailer was known for his pugnacious personality. He stabbed his wife Adele Morales with a penknife at a party to launch his mayoral campaign, after she supposedly told him he wasn't as good as Dostoevsky. James Baldwin compared this event to 'burning down the house in order to be free of it.' His feud with Gore Vidal culminated in an iconic appearance on The Dick Cavett Show, where Cavett told him to 'fold it five ways and put it where the moon don't shine' and Mailer punched Vidal (told 'once again, words have failed' him). Kate Millett mocked him as 'a prisoner of the virility cult' in Sexual Politics, to which he responded with The Prisoner of Sex. He called Michiko Kakutani 'a one-woman kamikaze.' He wrote the book The Fight about the 'Rumble in the Jungle' (covered in the documentary When We Were Kings). He also wrote The Faith of Graffiti with photographer John Naar, The Castle in the Forest (won the Bad Sex in Fiction Award), The Gospel According to the Son (framed as autobiography of Jesus), Harlot's Ghost (about the CIA), and 'Superman Comes to the Supermarket' about JFK's rise. He bit off a chunk of Rip Torn's ear during a fight on the set of Maidstone. Letters addressed to him make up Jack Henry Abbott's In the Belly of the Beast. Town Bloody Hall (Pennebaker documentary) depicts his debates with Germaine Greer.

clues.clue: Stabbed wife Adele Morales at party; told he wasn't as good as Dostoevsky
examples: supposedly told that he was not as good as Dostoyevsky, leading him to use a penknife to stab his wife Adele Morales
Baldwin compared this event to 'burning down the house in order, at last, to be free of it'

clues.clue: Feud with Gore Vidal; punched Vidal; Dick Cavett told him 'fold it five ways and put it where the moon don't shine'

clues.clue: Kate Millett called him 'prisoner of the virility cult' in Sexual Politics; responded with The Prisoner of Sex

The Prisoner of Sex was Mailer's book-length response to second-wave feminists

clues.clue: Called Kakutani 'a one-woman kamikaze'; wrote Gospel According to the Son (autobiography of Jesus)
examples: called Kakutani a 'one-woman kamikaze'

clues.clue: Letters to him make up In the Belly of the Beast; Town Bloody Hall documentary with Germaine Greer

clues.clue: 'Superman Comes to the Supermarket' about JFK's rise
examples: wrote the New Journalism piece 'Superman Comes to the Supermarket', which was about this American figure's rise to prominence

comprehensive_summary:
Norman Mailer was an American novelist, journalist, and public intellectual, a central figure of New Journalism known for his pugnacious personality and prolific output.

His first novel, The Naked and the Dead, draws on his WWII experience in the Philippines and depicts an American regiment's invasion of the fictional island Anopopei. It uses the euphemism 'fug,' prompting Dorothy Parker to call Mailer 'the man who can't spell fuck.' Sergeant Croft crushes a baby bird, kills a POW, and orders an attack on Mount Anaka.

The Armies of the Night is a nonfiction novel about the 1967 March on the Pentagon, split into 'History as a Novel' and 'The Novel as History.' Mailer joins friend Mitch Goodman and marches alongside Dwight Macdonald and Robert Lowell.

The Executioner's Song, winner of the Pulitzer Prize, chronicles the capital punishment of Gary Gilmore in Utah, who murders two men and demands his death penalty. Nicole Baker has a brief affair with him.

'The White Negro: Superficial Reflections on the Hipster' discusses how existentialist 'philosophical psychopaths' absorbed black culture after WWII, describes jazz as 'the music of orgasm,' and was collected in Advertisements for Myself. The CIA kept a copy on file. James Baldwin criticized Mailer's appropriation of black language.

An American Dream features Stephen Rojack murdering his wife and having sex with her maid. Ancient Evenings is set in Egypt about the thrice-reincarnated Menenhetet, charioteer for Ramses II.

Mailer stabbed his wife Adele Morales at a party launching his mayoral campaign after being told he wasn't as good as Dostoevsky. His feud with Gore Vidal culminated on The Dick Cavett Show, where he punched Vidal and was told 'once again, words have failed you.' Kate Millett attacked him as 'a prisoner of the virility cult' in Sexual Politics; he responded with The Prisoner of Sex. He wrote The Fight about the Rumble in the Jungle, bit Rip Torn's ear on the set of Maidstone, and wrote 'Superman Comes to the Supermarket' about JFK. Letters to him comprise Jack Henry Abbott's In the Belly of the Beast. The Pennebaker documentary Town Bloody Hall depicts his debates with Germaine Greer. He won the Bad Sex in Fiction Award for The Castle in the Forest.
